name: BIOCRYST PHARMACEUTICALS INC
business: BioCryst is a global biotechnology company focused on developing and commercializing medicines for hereditary angioedema (HAE) and other rare diseases. Its lead product ORLADEYO is an oral once-daily therapy for prophylaxis of HAE attacks, approved in the US and other markets. The company also has a pipeline including navenibart (Phase 3 for HAE), BCX17725 (Phase 1 for Netherton syndrome), avoralstat (Phase 1 for diabetic macular edema), STAR-0310 (Phase 1a for atopic dermatitis), and approved peramivir for influenza.
sellsTo: Patients with hereditary angioedema and other rare diseases
Collaboration partners in Japan, Europe, Latin America, Middle East, and Asia

exposures.sentence: ORLADEYO is an oral, once-daily therapy discovered and developed by us for the prevention of HAE attacks.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of ORLADEYO oral pellet formulation for pediatric patients aged 2 to 12
catalysts.sentence: On December 12, 2025, we announced that the FDA approved our new drug application for the use of an oral pellet formulation of once-daily ORLADEYO for prophylactic therapy in pediatric patients with HAE aged 2 to 12 years.

On January 23, 2026 (the Closing Date), BioCryst completed the transactions contemplated by the Agreement and Plan of Merger, dated as of October 14, 2025, by and among BioCryst, Axel Merger Sub, Inc., a Delaware corporation and a wholly owned subsidiary of BioCryst (Merger Sub) and Astria Therapeutics, Inc., a Delaware corporation (Astria).

catalysts.event: Positive interim results from ALPHA SOLAR trial for navenibart
catalysts.sentence: On February 26, 2026, we announced that new positive, interim results from the long term, open label ALPHA SOLAR trial show sustained, robust HAE attack suppression with navenibart administered every three and six months.

catalysts.event: Expect to report data from BCX17725 clinical trial for Netherton syndrome
catalysts.sentence: On February 26, 2026, we announced that we expect to report data from the clinical trial of BCX17725 for the treatment of Netherton syndrome in up to 12 patients by the end of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global peak annual net ORLADEYO revenues of $1 billion
tamClaims.sentence: Based on proprietary analyses of HAE prevalence and market research studies with HAE patients, physicians, and payors in the United States and Europe, and five full years of commercialization experience with ORLADEYO from 2021 through 2025, we anticipate that the global commercial market for ORLADEYO has the potential to reach a global peak of $1 billion in annual net ORLADEYO revenues.
